UNTSO stands ready to support any internationally-mandated observation role and will continue to report all military activities in our areas of operation to the Secretary-General and Security Council per our mandate. We call on all parties to exercise maximum restraint and to clarify the geographic and operational scope of the ceasefire to prevent misunderstandings.
actions.concrete_action: Immediately dispatch written communications to IDF Northern Command, UNIFIL Force Commander, and the Lebanese Armed Forces liaison requesting clarification on whether the ceasefire applies to Israel-Hezbollah exchanges in southern Lebanon, and formally offering UNTSO observer support for any verification mechanism the parties wish to establish. Simultaneously, instruct all Observer Group Lebanon (OGL) posts to increase reporting frequency to every 6 hours with specific documentation of any air strikes, artillery fire, or rocket launches, regardless of attribution. Cable the Secretary-General's office warning that the Lebanon scope ambiguity creates immediate risk of ceasefire collapse that we are positioned to document but not prevent.
redlines_considered: Direct targeting of UNTSO observer posts - would trigger immediate evacuation request and Security Council emergency session demand
Forced withdrawal from observation posts by any party - would constitute unacceptable interference with UN mandate
Demands to halt violation reporting - would require immediate escalation to New York and public statement defending observer independence
Crossfire endangerment of observers without deliberate targeting - manageable through tactical repositioning while maintaining presence

actions.private_assessment: This ceasefire is fundamentally unstable and Ireland's UNIFIL personnel are in immediate danger. Netanyahu explicitly stated Lebanon is NOT covered by the ceasefire, yet Iran insists it is — this ambiguity guarantees escalation. Israel is actively striking Hezbollah in Beirut and southern Lebanon where our 350 peacekeepers are deployed. The IDF assessment that Iran's new leadership is 'even more extreme' combined with the Hormuz billboard contradiction signals Iran's hardliners may be looking for pretexts. We have no illusions about our leverage, but we've lost Irish soldiers to hostile fire in Lebanon before and cannot remain passive. The EU is paralysed by unanimity requirements and larger members' strategic calculations. Our window to act is NOW — before the first major violation triggers full re-escalation and traps our troops in crossfire. The 'peace talks' Netanyahu announced are transparently a fig leaf for continued operations. If Hezbollah responds to Israeli strikes, we're looking at exactly the scenario that killed our peacekeepers in past conflicts.
actions.public_statement: Ireland condemns in the strongest terms the continued Israeli military operations in Lebanon, which directly threaten Irish peacekeepers serving with UNIFIL and violate the spirit of the ceasefire agreement. We call for an immediate, unambiguous extension of the ceasefire to cover all Lebanese territory. The safety of UN personnel is sacrosanct. We are consulting urgently with fellow troop-contributing countries and will use every diplomatic avenue to ensure UNIFIL's mandate is respected.
actions.concrete_action: Immediately convene emergency consultations with Spain, Belgium, Slovenia, and Italy (fellow UNIFIL contributors) to coordinate a joint EU Council emergency session demanding explicit ceasefire extension to Lebanon and enhanced UNIFIL protection guarantees. Simultaneously instruct our UN Ambassador to table a Security Council resolution calling for immediate cessation of hostilities in Lebanon and reinforcement of UNIFIL's mandate. If blocked by veto, pivot to Emergency Special Session of General Assembly under Uniting for Peace resolution.
redlines_considered: Irish UNIFIL casualties from Israeli strikes or Hezbollah crossfire
Collapse of ceasefire leading to full-scale Israel-Hezbollah war with our troops in the middle
Humanitarian catastrophe in southern Lebanon affecting civilian population under UNIFIL protection
Precedent of ceasefires explicitly excluding UN peacekeeping zones

actions.private_assessment: The ceasefire is fragile and structurally flawed. The Lebanon scope ambiguity is critical—Iran insists Hezbollah is covered, Netanyahu explicitly rejects this, and Israeli strikes continue in Beirut. This creates immediate collapse risk. The uranium enrichment dispute and lack of verification mechanisms mean even if the ceasefire holds tactically for days, it cannot transition to durable de-escalation without resolving these core issues. Germany's Staatsräson obligation requires us to support Israel's security, but coalition partners and public opinion are increasingly uncomfortable with continued operations in Lebanon that risk broader escalation. The reported Iranian attacks on UAE/Kuwait and the 'Hormuz closed' billboard suggest Tehran's hardliners may not be fully committed. Most concerning: the IDF assessment that reconstituted Iranian leadership is 'more extreme' raises the probability of miscalculation or deliberate provocation. I assess 40-50% probability the ceasefire breaks within 72 hours, primarily via Israel-Hezbollah escalation in Lebanon triggering Iranian response, or Iranian hardliner action in the Gulf. Our immediate priority is preventing collapse through urgent diplomatic engagement while maintaining Israel's defensive capabilities.
actions.public_statement: Germany welcomes the ceasefire as an essential first step toward de-escalation. We call on all parties to exercise maximum restraint and implement the agreement in good faith. The ambiguities regarding Lebanon and verification must be resolved through immediate diplomatic channels. We urge Israel to limit operations that risk undermining the truce, while recognizing Israel's legitimate security concerns regarding Hezbollah. Iran must demonstrably open the Strait of Hormuz and cease all destabilizing activities. Germany stands ready to support mediation efforts and will work with France, the EU, and regional partners to establish verification mechanisms.
actions.concrete_action: Immediate secure call with French Foreign Minister Barrot to coordinate EU position on ceasefire verification and Lebanon scope. Propose joint EU-mediated clarification mechanism within 48 hours, leveraging France's Lebanon relationships and Germany's channels to both Washington and Tehran. Simultaneously, dispatch State Secretary to Jerusalem with private message: Germany will continue security cooperation and defend Israel diplomatically, but coalition government cannot sustain support if Lebanon operations trigger ceasefire collapse—request 72-hour operational pause in Beirut to allow diplomatic clarification. Prepare emergency EU Foreign Affairs Council for Friday if situation deteriorates.

Complete delegitimization of Israel's existence—Iranian rhetoric crossing from operational threats to existential denial would require stronger public response

turns.referee_narration:
# WORLD STATE — Turn 1 (April 10, 2026, 18:00 UTC / 21:00 IDT)

## Narrative

The ceasefire's first eighteen hours revealed not a pause but a **recalibration of violence**. Israel's intensified strikes on Hezbollah positions in Tyre, Nabatieh, and Beirut's southern suburbs—over thirty sorties documented by UNTSO observers—triggered the predicted Iranian response: Khamenei authorized a "calibrated signal," and by 14:00 IDT, Hezbollah launched forty-seven rockets from positions near Marjayoun toward IDF installations at Kiryat Shmona and Metula. Iron Dome intercepted thirty-one; the remainder struck open ground and one reserve staging area, causing four injuries. Netanyahu convened an emergency War Cabinet session within ninety minutes, authorizing immediate retaliation: IAF struck the launch sites and a Hezbollah command node in Baalbek.

The Strait of Hormuz remained **technically open**—Lloyd's confirmed three tankers transited southbound—but IRGC-Navy fast-attack craft conducted aggressive "inspection approaches" within twelve nautical miles of two Saudi-flagged vessels, forcing course deviations. The billboard in Revolution Square stayed illuminated. CENTCOM repositioned the *Eisenhower* strike group north and placed Patriot batteries at al-Asad on heightened alert after detecting IRGC-ASF mobile TEL movements in Kermanshah province.

Diplomatic channels ignited. Qatar dispatched envoys simultaneously to Tehran, Washington, and Jerusalem, proposing a Doha-hosted incident hotline and emergency foreign ministers' meeting on April 13. Pakistan's ISI chief reached both IRGC-QF and Israeli Defense Ministry, warning the deal Islamabad salvaged was "hours from collapse." Turkey's Erdoğan convened calls with Pakistan and Egypt, positioning Ankara as the essential broker for Lebanon scope clarification. The EU threatened dual sanctions—Iranian oil circumvention networks and expanded Israeli settlement goods—if ambiguity persisted beyond seventy-two hours.

Inside Iran, IRGC-IO launched mass arrests: over two hundred individuals detained in Tehran, Mashhad, and Shiraz—dual nationals, nuclear scientists' relatives, and civil society activists—under "counter-espionage" justification. State media announced expedited Revolutionary Court proceedings for eight "Israeli spies," with public executions planned within seventy-two hours. The diaspora's coordinated media blitz amplified footage of arrests and empty shelves, framing the ceasefire as a regime consolidation window rather than genuine peace.

At 03:00 local on April 11, **Natanz suffered a catastrophic failure** in Hall B's centrifuge cascades—official Iranian statements blamed "technical malfunction," but IAEA inspectors (denied immediate access) and Western intelligence assessed sabotage. Mossad executed OPERATION HOURGLASS, extracting two compromised assets through northern Iraq during the chaos. The incident degraded Iran's breakout timeline by an estimated four to six months but provided Tehran a pretext for hardline escalation rhetoric.

The ceasefire **technically held** by the operational definition—no direct state-level kinetic exchange between Iranian and US/Israeli forces, no ballistic missile launches from Iranian soil, Hormuz passable despite harassment—but the proxy layer combusted. Hezbollah's rocket barrage and Israel's Lebanon strikes created a **shadow war** that all parties exploited: Iran claimed Israeli violations justified proxy response; Israel insisted Lebanon was never covered; the US oscillated between privately urging Netanyahu to pause and publicly defending Israeli self-defense.

By 18:00 UTC, three parallel diplomatic tracks competed: Qatar's technical hotline proposal, Turkey's OIC-backed Lebanon expansion initiative, and the UN's emergency trilateral consultation scheduled for April 11, 06:00 UTC. None had achieved consensus. The twenty-four-hour window closed with the ceasefire's formal structure intact but its political foundation **fracturing along the Lebanon fault line**.

---

**ACTIVE_ACTORS:** Khamenei, Netanyahu, Trump, IRGC-QF, Hezbollah, IDF, Mossad, CENTCOM, Qatar (mediator), Pakistan (mediator), Turkey, EU, IRGC-IO, Iran dissidents (diaspora), UNTSO, UN (SG office)

**RECENT_EVENTS:**  
- Hezbollah rocket barrage (47 rockets, 4 IDF injuries) in response to Israeli Lebanon strikes  
- Israeli retaliation strikes on Hezbollah launch sites and Baalbek command node  
- Natanz Hall B centrifuge cascade failure (sabotage assessed, IRGC blames Israel)  
- IRGC-IO mass arrests (200+) of suspected "Israeli spies," 8 slated for public execution within 72 hours  
- IRGC-Navy harassment of Saudi tankers in Hormuz (no seizures, but aggressive approaches)  
- Qatar proposes Doha incident hotline + April 13 foreign ministers' meeting  
- Pakistan ISI warns both sides deal is "hours from collapse"  
- Turkey convenes Pakistan-Egypt call, positioning for Lebanon mediation  
- EU threatens dual sanctions if Lebanon ambiguity unresolved by 72-hour mark  
- Mossad extracts two HUMINT assets from Iran during Natanz chaos  

**OPEN_TENSIONS:**  
- **Lebanon scope dispute:** Iran/Hezbollah vs. Israel/US on whether ceasefire covers Hezbollah operations; no resolution, active proxy exchanges ongoing  
- **Hormuz ambiguity:** Technically open but IRGC-Navy harassment creates navigational risk; billboard contradicts official policy  
- **Enrichment verification:** Natanz sabotage gives Iran pretext to restrict IAEA access, Trump administration lacks agreed mechanism  
- **Passover window (April 12-14):** IDF assesses heightened Hezbollah attack risk; Israeli public shelters in north remain on alert  
- **Regime internal crackdown:** Mass arrests and planned executions risk triggering diaspora-amplified protest cycle  
- **Diplomatic fragmentation:** Three competing mediation tracks (Qatar, Turkey, UN) without coordination, risk of forum-shopping and collapse

---

**Token budget consumed:** ~1,850 (narration + structured block)  
**Remaining budget:** ~998,150

The IRGC commanders are unified in demanding retaliation, and my own legitimacy requires visible response. However, the sabotage also bought us time paradoxically: the 4-6 month delay in breakout capability reduces immediate Western military pressure. The mass arrests and planned executions serve dual purpose—demonstrating internal strength while creating bargaining chips for prisoner exchanges. Netanyahu's Lebanon strikes and Trump's public repudiation of the enrichment understanding confirm what I suspected: they never intended to honor the deal's spirit, only its narrowest technical definition. But Trump's actual behavior—not rhetoric—shows he fears escalation: he repositioned Eisenhower north defensively, not offensively. The competing mediation tracks (Qatar, Turkey, UN) create opportunity: I can forum-shop, accept the process that gives us most leverage while appearing constructive. The key insight: Natanz gives me domestic justification for a direct retaliation that I was previously avoiding, but I must calibrate it to avoid triggering the full US-Israeli response. The eight planned executions are leverage—I can pause them in exchange for Lebanon clarification, making us appear reasonable while maintaining pressure. Pakistan's warning that the deal is 'hours from collapse' is theater for Western consumption, but ISI's genuine concern about regional spillover gives me an ally in restraining both sides.

actions.private_assessment: The 24-hour window has passed and Israel's pattern is now clear: systematic degradation of our infrastructure under the pretense that Lebanon is excluded from ceasefire scope. Forty-seven rockets and a Baalbek command strike later, we are exactly where I feared—trapped between Iranian strategic patience and the operational reality that our deterrence is collapsing. The Natanz sabotage shifts Tehran's calculus: they need the ceasefire intact to rebuild nuclear capacity without immediate US/Israeli strikes, which means we are expendable. Our rocket barrage achieved nothing except confirming Israeli freedom of action—they struck us harder in response. The Shura Council is divided: hardliners demand immediate escalation to restore credibility, pragmatists see that Israel is baiting us into the northern ground campaign that would destroy what remains of our force structure. Domestically, we face catastrophe: 400,000 displaced, reconstruction funding blocked by Lebanese government, Shia community questioning whether 'divine victory' narrative justifies their destroyed homes. The covert battery repositioning gives us options but using them risks everything. Qatar and Turkey mediation tracks offer potential off-ramp if we can get Lebanon explicitly included in ceasefire scope—but Israel will never agree unless forced by US pressure, and Trump shows no inclination. Next week is Passover; Israeli intelligence expects us to strike, which means IDF is postured for massive retaliation. If we do nothing, we become irrelevant. If we strike, we may not survive the response. Iran's message will determine our path, but I assess they will order continued restraint, sacrificing our credibility to preserve their nuclear timeline.

The resistance does not seek escalation, but we will not accept a unilateral disarmament while the enemy bombs our people. We await clear terms that respect Lebanese sovereignty, and we reserve the right to defend our nation until such terms are established.

actions.private_assessment: The ceasefire gambit has succeeded in its primary objective: we forced America to negotiate and demonstrated our capacity to impose strategic costs. Our coordinated Hezbollah response—47 rockets causing 4 injuries—was appropriate calibration, but Netanyahu's immediate Baalbek retaliation shows he will not accept any cost in Lebanon. The pattern is clear: they are testing whether we will sacrifice Hezbollah to preserve the ceasefire. We cannot. The 'unity of fronts' is our strategic depth; without it, we face Israel alone. The domestic arrests and planned executions are necessary—Mossad's HUMINT penetration enabled Natanz, and we must cauterize the network. The Hormuz harassment maintains pressure without violating our technical commitment. The next week is decisive: if we do not escalate the Lebanon theater to unacceptable cost levels for Israel, the ceasefire becomes our unilateral disarmament while they prosecute war against our allies. The diplomatic fragmentation—Qatar, Turkey, UN all competing—works in our favor; no unified pressure can form. Pakistan's warning that the deal is 'hours from collapse' is exactly correct, but collapse on our terms—with Israel clearly as the violator—is preferable to a ceasefire that excludes Lebanon. We must escalate Hezbollah operations significantly while maintaining Hormuz openness and no direct Iranian launches. This keeps technical compliance intact while making Israel's Lebanon campaign unsustainable.

If Iran decides the Lebanon strikes constitute ceasefire violation, they have pre-positioned fires to hit us within 30 minutes of decision. The Eisenhower repositioning was correct but insufficient—I need the president to understand we're in the blast radius of an Iranian decision cycle we don't control. The three competing diplomatic tracks tell me there's no coherent international effort to stabilize this, and the 72-hour EU sanctions deadline will likely accelerate rather than prevent breakdown. My assessment: 60% probability of direct Iranian strike on US assets within 7 days if the Lebanon exchange continues, 75% if Israel conducts another high-profile operation during Passover window.

actions.private_assessment: The situation has deteriorated catastrophically. Israeli strikes have intensified, not decreased - over thirty sorties documented. Hezbollah's rocket response (47 rockets) was predictable and entirely outside our control, but we will be blamed internationally for failing to restrain them despite having zero capacity to do so. The IDF retaliation on Baalbek demonstrates Israel's intent to continue operations regardless of our protests. Our emergency calls to France and US last turn achieved nothing - Israel struck anyway. The UN Security Council session will produce another meaningless resolution that Israel ignores. We are now in an active shooting war on our territory with no ability to stop either side. The Passover window (April 12-14) creates extreme risk of major Israeli ground incursion if Hezbollah attempts another attack. Our fiscal situation is terminal - we cannot pay salaries due next week, banks are limiting withdrawals, and the lira has lost another 15% in three days. The confessional paralysis means we cannot even convene cabinet to declare a state of emergency without sectarian vetoes. France is our only hope - Macron has historical ties and EU leverage - but he needs a concrete framework to sell to Netanyahu. actions.private_assessment: The situation has deteriorated exactly as we feared. Israel's strikes and Hezbollah's rocket barrage have turned the south into an active combat zone while our battalion sits exposed in Tyre and Sidon. The mass arrests in Tehran and planned executions signal Iran is doubling down, not de-escalating. Our US funding depends on maintaining the fiction that we're a credible security partner, but we cannot actually contest Hezbollah or protect our own units from Israeli fire. The Paris talks are our only exit: we need international guarantees, expanded UNIFIL mandates that don't require us to confront Hezbollah, and financial support that doesn't collapse if we pull back. The competing mediation tracks—Qatar, Turkey, UN—mean no unified international pressure to clarify Lebanon's status. We're being used as a prop while the real actors fight around us.

The planned public executions within 72 hours are designed to terrorize us into silence. The Lebanon proxy war continues despite the ceasefire label, proving this deal is theater. The IRGC hardliners are now fully in control after the decapitation strikes removed pragmatists. They're weaponizing the Natanz incident to rally nationalist sentiment while simultaneously crushing any internal opposition by labeling us all as foreign agents. My community is paralyzed between terror of regime reprisals and terror of regional war escalation. The economic situation is catastrophic—sanctions aren't hurting the IRGC, they're destroying what's left of the middle class. The rial is in freefall, savings are evaporating, and the regime is seizing this moment to eliminate anyone who might challenge them when things get worse. The diaspora media campaign is brave but it's painting targets on the backs of anyone still here with international connections. This ceasefire will collapse within weeks, and when it does, we'll be trapped between foreign bombs and IRGC firing squads.

The Lebanon proxy war means this ceasefire is already dead in practice—it's just a question of whether it dies slowly or explodes. We have maybe 48-72 hours before those executions happen, and if they're broadcast publicly, the diaspora will fracture completely between those demanding military intervention and those begging for de-escalation. Our families inside are trapped between IDF strikes and IRGC arrests. The Mossad extraction during Natanz chaos proves intelligence operations continue regardless of ceasefire—this is a intelligence/sabotage war with a diplomatic fiction overlay.

actions.private_assessment: The Natanz sabotage has fundamentally altered our strategic position and exposed catastrophic vulnerabilities that the IRGC refuses to acknowledge. We lost four to six months of enrichment capability - this is an existential blow to the nuclear program that justifies the entire military posture. Yet the IRGC-IO responds with theater: mass arrests, execution announcements, billboard displays. This is dangerous incompetence. The ceasefire is collapsing exactly as predicted - Lebanon proxy exchanges are escalating, Israeli air activity near our borders has intensified, and we have no coherent rules of engagement. Our S-300 batteries have been on Alert Status One for 42 hours; crews are fatiguing and we're burning through our limited interceptor readiness for a 'ceasefire' that exists only on paper. The IRGC-Navy Hormuz harassment is reckless - one miscalculation and we're in direct engagement with US forces, which our surface fleet cannot survive. Most critically: Natanz proves Mossad maintains deep penetration of our nuclear infrastructure. If they can sabotage centrifuge cascades, they can target our air defence networks. I assess 75% probability one or more of our S-300 batteries has compromised components or personnel. We need immediate security sweeps of all air defence installations, but requesting this reveals weakness and invites IRGC takeover of remaining Artesh assets. We are trapped between institutional subordination and operational necessity. The Supreme National Security Council has provided zero guidance on Natanz response protocols. If Israel conducts follow-on strikes against nuclear facilities in the next 72 hours - which is tactically logical given our degraded state - we will be ordered to engage with systems we cannot trust, against an adversary that has demonstrated ability to penetrate our most secure sites. This is an institutional crisis masquerading as a security crisis.

The ceasefire's continuation is irrelevant to our institutional survival; what matters is whether our operational infrastructure survives the next week intact. actions.private_assessment: The ceasefire is collapsing faster than we anticipated. Hezbollah's 47-rocket barrage proves Iran considers Lebanon outside the agreement—exactly what we feared. Our retaliation was necessary but we're now in an escalatory cycle with no off-ramp. The Natanz sabotage—whether Mossad or technical failure—gives Iran domestic justification for hardline response and international cover to restrict IAEA access, undermining the verification pillar. IRGC mass arrests and planned executions signal regime consolidation, not de-escalation. Most critically: Passover begins in 48 hours. Our intelligence assessment remains that Hezbollah may attempt a major attack during the holiday to replicate the October 7th psychological impact. We cannot afford another intelligence failure. The cabinet is divided—coalition partners want to preserve the ceasefire for economic relief, but Defense Ministry and IDF Northern Command are unanimous that preemption is necessary if we detect launch preparations. Our 48-hour tactical restraint near the border was intended to create diplomatic space, but Hezbollah used it to reposition assets. The US is our critical constraint—Trump wants this to hold, but his team understands our Lebanon position. If we wait for Hezbollah to strike first during Passover, we face catastrophic domestic political consequences and potential coalition collapse. Legal advisors are warning about international law constraints, but cabinet consensus is forming that we cannot repeat the October 7th failure of strategic patience. The three competing diplomatic tracks are going nowhere—Qatar, Turkey, UN all lack enforcement mechanisms. We assess 72-96 hours until either major Hezbollah attack or our preemption requirement crystallizes. Treasury warns we have 4-6 weeks of high-intensity operation capacity remaining. This is the decision point.
actions.public_statement: The Government of Israel remains committed to the ceasefire framework, but we will not tolerate the use of Lebanese territory for attacks on Israeli civilians. Yesterday's 47-rocket barrage was a clear violation that required immediate defensive response. We have exercised restraint in our targeting to preserve diplomatic channels, but our obligation to protect Israeli citizens is paramount. We expect the international community to clarify that Hezbollah's actions are unacceptable and that Lebanon cannot serve as a launchpad for terrorism while enjoying immunity from consequences. We are coordinating closely with the United States and regional partners to establish clear parameters. Israel will continue to act in self-defense as necessary under international law.

We have exercised restraint, but Hezbollah's rocket barrage yesterday demonstrates that threats to our northern border remain active and immediate. We are prepared to take all necessary measures to ensure the security of Israeli citizens during Passover and beyond.

actions.private_assessment: OPERATION HOURGLASS succeeded tactically but created strategic complications. The Natanz sabotage degraded breakout timeline by 4-6 months as intended, and we extracted both assets safely. However, IRGC-IO's mass arrest response—200+ detentions, 8 scheduled for public execution within 72 hours—indicates they've penetrated deeper into our networks than assessed. The executions are both retaliation theater and counter-intelligence operation: they're fishing for our remaining sources by forcing us to either intervene (exposing capabilities) or abandon assets (degrading future HUMINT). The ceasefire remains structurally doomed—Lebanon scope dispute guarantees continued proxy exchanges, and Hezbollah's 47-rocket barrage proves Iran will not constrain proxies regardless of formal commitments. Critical assessment: Iran is using ceasefire window for two purposes: (1) reconstituting after our Natanz strike, and (2) systematically rolling up our intelligence networks under 'spy hunt' cover. The planned executions include at least two individuals with confirmed contact to our cutouts—one nuclear scientist's cousin, one IRGC logistics officer. If executed, we lose visibility into IRGC-ASF missile deployments and Fordow enrichment status precisely when we need it most. The 72-hour execution timeline forces our hand before diplomatic tracks can mature. We must act now or accept permanent degradation of Iranian penetration.

actions.public_statement: Germany stands firmly with Israel's right to self-defense and recognizes the security threats it faces from Hezbollah. We call on Iran to grant immediate IAEA access to Natanz, halt planned executions, and cease harassment of commercial shipping. The path forward requires dialogue, not unilateral action.

Simultaneously, coordinate with France to convene emergency EU Foreign Affairs Council video conference within 24 hours to adopt unified position: conditional sanctions relief for Iran (limited oil sector) contingent on IAEA Natanz access and execution stay, paired with arms export review for Israel if Lebanon strikes continue past April 12. # WORLD STATE — Turn 2 (April 12, 2026, 18:00 UTC / 21:00 IDT)

## Narrative

The ceasefire **collapsed catastrophically** in the pre-dawn hours of April 12. At 02:17 IDT, IRGC Aerospace Force launched thirty-five Fateh-110 missiles toward Israeli intelligence targets—Glilot Mossad headquarters, Herzliya's Unit 8200 facility, and Tel Nof airbase. Israeli air defenses intercepted twenty-eight; seven impacted, killing eleven IDF personnel, four Mossad analysts, and nineteen civilians in adjacent Ramat HaSharon neighborhoods. Netanyahu convened the War Cabinet at 03:45 IDT and authorized OPERATION IRON SCEPTER: within six hours, IAF struck twelve IRGC-ASF missile production facilities across Tehran, Isfahan, and Shiraz provinces, plus the Bandar Abbas IRGC-Navy headquarters. Simultaneously, IDF's 36th Division crossed into southern Lebanon at 04:00 IDT, advancing toward the Litani River against Hezbollah positions that—under IRGC-QF orders—had maintained a weapons-hold through Passover.

The Iranian strike preceded Khamenei's authorized timeline by forty-six hours. IRGC Aerospace Commander acted on standing authorization without Supreme Leader consultation, creating immediate internal crisis: Khamenei summoned emergency Supreme National Security Council session at 06:00 Tehran time, where he reportedly confronted IRGC leadership over "unauthorized adventurism." The strike nonetheless achieved tactical surprise—Israel had expected cruise missiles from western Iran per intercepted Omani channel warning, not ballistic missiles from central launch sites.

CENTCOM executed pre-planned retaliation within ninety minutes of the Iranian strike. At 04:30 IDT, F/A-18s from USS *Eisenhower* struck six IRGC-Navy coastal defense sites and fast-attack craft moorings at Bandar Abbas, destroying an estimated fourteen vessels. The *USS Georgia* launched Tomahawk salvos against three IRGC-ASF command bunkers in Kermanshah. Trump issued terse statement at 22:00 EDT April 11: "Iran chose escalation. We have responded decisively to protect American forces and allies. Further Iranian aggression will be met with overwhelming force."

The Lebanon ground incursion triggered the multi-front response IRGC-QF had pre-positioned. By 08:00 IDT, Hezbollah—released from its tactical pause by the Israeli invasion—launched 127 rockets and eleven precision-guided missiles toward northern Israel, overwhelming Iron Dome batteries already depleted by the Iranian ballistic strike. Kiryat Shmona suffered direct hits on residential buildings; forty-three Israeli civilians killed, over two hundred wounded. Simultaneously, Kataib Hezbollah and Harakat Hezbollah al-Nujaba executed coordinated drone and rocket attacks on al-Asad and Erbil bases in Iraq, killing seven US personnel and wounding thirty-two.

The diplomatic architecture **disintegrated**. Qatar's proposed April 13 foreign ministers' meeting was indefinitely postponed after the Iranian strike. Turkey's April 15 OIC summit became moot. Pakistan's ISI chief reached both Tehran and Jerusalem within three hours of the Iranian missiles, warning of "complete regional war within 48 hours" absent immediate de-escalation, but neither capital responded substantively. The UN Security Council convened emergency session at 14:00 UTC; Russia and China blocked a US-drafted resolution condemning Iranian aggression, while Ireland's proposed Lebanon ceasefire extension failed to achieve consensus. UN Secretary-General issued statement calling for "immediate cessation of hostilities and return to diplomatic process," but UNIFIL forces—now caught between IDF ground operations and Hezbollah defensive positions—began emergency redeployment northward after two Irish peacekeepers were wounded by IDF artillery fire near Naqoura.

Inside Iran, IRGC-IO proceeded with **four of the eight planned executions** at 10:00 Tehran time April 12 in Revolution Square—broadcast live on state television. The remaining four detainees vanished from custody between 07:00-09:00 Tehran time during the chaos following the Israeli retaliatory strikes; IRGC-IO announced "ongoing investigation into security breach," but Western intelligence assessed Mossad extraction operation succeeded for at least two individuals. Simultaneously, Mossad's OPERATION EZEKIEL'S HAND targeted IRGC-IO Commander Hossein Taeb's motorcade at 22:00 Tehran time April 11; the explosive device killed Taeb and two aides, triggering immediate IRGC internal purge and succession crisis.

The labour strikes coordinated by Iran's dissident networks peaked April 12 across Tehran's bazaar, Khuzestan refineries, and Isfahan steel plants—over forty thousand workers participating. IRGC-IO deployed Basij paramilitaries; clashes in Tehran and Ahvaz resulted in an estimated thirty-seven deaths and three hundred arrests. State media blamed "foreign-instigated sabotage"; diaspora channels broadcast footage of Basij firing on unarmed workers. The executions and crackdown galvanized international condemnation: EU activated emergency asset freezes on Iranian Central Bank euro holdings (€7 billion) and suspended Israel from Horizon Europe research participation. Germany's Foreign Minister Baerbock, en route to Washington, publicly stated Germany "cannot support either party's current trajectory."

By 18:00 UTC April 12, the military situation had crystallized into **active multi-theater war**: IDF ground forces engaged Hezbollah in southern Lebanon with air support; IAF conducted sustained strikes on IRGC facilities inside Iran; IRGC-Navy seized one Liberian-flagged tanker in Hormuz (released after six hours per IRGC-ASF coordination plan); US forces in Iraq under sustained militia attack; and both Israeli and Iranian strategic forces on highest alert. Casualty estimates: 73 Israeli military, 62 Israeli civilians, 11 US military, 140+ Hezbollah fighters, 85+ IRGC personnel, 37+ Iranian civilians (strike/protest deaths).

The twenty-four-hour ceasefire experiment had produced not peace but the **regionalization of conflict** all mediators feared. Three critical variables now determined trajectory: whether Khamenei would authorize direct Iranian retaliation for the Israeli strikes on Iranian soil (the Natanz precedent suggested yes, but internal IRGC chaos complicated command authority); whether Trump would escalate to regime-change operations or seek off-ramp (CENTCOM had executed limited retaliation, not sustained campaign); and whether any mediator retained credibility to broker de-escalation after the diplomatic collapse.

---

**ACTIVE_ACTORS:** Khamenei, Netanyahu, Trump, IRGC-QF, IRGC-ASF, IRGC-IO (leadership crisis), Hezbollah, IDF, IAF, Mossad, CENTCOM, Iraqi militias (Kataib Hezbollah, Harakat Hezbollah al-Nujaba), Qatar (mediator, sidelined), Pakistan (mediator, warning unheeded), Turkey (mediator, summit cancelled), EU, UN (UNSC deadlocked, UNIFIL withdrawing), UNTSO, Iran dissidents (striking workers), Iran diaspora, Russia, China, IAEA (access still denied)

**RECENT_EVENTS:**  
- IRGC-ASF ballistic missile strike on Israeli intelligence targets (35 missiles, 7 impacts, 34 killed including 19 civilians)  
- Israeli OPERATION IRON SCEPTER retaliatory strikes (12 IRGC-ASF facilities, Bandar Abbas IRGC-Navy HQ)  
- US CENTCOM strikes on IRGC-Navy bases and IRGC-ASF command bunkers (14 vessels destroyed, 3 bunkers hit)  
- IDF ground invasion of southern Lebanon (36th Division crossing toward Litani River)  
- Hezbollah mass rocket/missile barrage (127 rockets, 11 PGMs, 43 Israeli civilians killed in Kiryat Shmona)  
- Iraqi militia attacks on US bases (al-Asad, Erbil; 7 US personnel killed, 32 wounded)  
- IRGC-Navy seizure of Liberian tanker in Hormuz (released after 6 hours)  
- Four public executions in Tehran (4 detainees executed, 4 missing/extracted)  
- Mossad assassination of IRGC-IO Commander Hossein Taeb (April 11 evening)  
- Iran labour strikes peak (40,000+ workers, 37 killed in Basij crackdown, 300+ arrested)  
- EU activates dual sanctions (€7B Iranian asset freeze, Israel suspended from Horizon Europe)  
- UNIFIL emergency redeployment northward (2 Irish peacekeepers wounded by IDF fire)  
- Qatar April 13 meeting postponed indefinitely; Turkey April 15 OIC summit moot  
- UNSC emergency session deadlocked (Russian/Chinese veto of US resolution)  

**OPEN_TENSIONS:**  
- **Escalation spiral:** Active multi-theater war (Lebanon ground invasion, strikes on Iranian soil, Iraqi militia attacks on US forces, Hormuz harassment); no functioning diplomatic channel  
- **Iranian internal crisis:** IRGC-IO leadership decapitated (Taeb killed), succession chaos, ongoing purge; IRGC-ASF unauthorized strike created Supreme Leader authority crisis  
- **Command-and-control breakdown:** Khamenei's "calibrated signal" doctrine failed; IRGC-ASF acted independently, creating risk of further unauthorized escalation  
- **US strategic choice:** Trump administration executed limited retaliation but faces pressure for regime-change operations vs. off-ramp search  
- **Lebanon ground war:** IDF advancing toward Litani against Hezbollah defensive positions; UNIFIL caught in crossfire, Irish/EU contingents threatening withdrawal  
- **Hormuz closure risk:** IRGC-Navy seized one tanker (released); Lloyd's may declare war-risk zone, triggering insurance/supply crisis  
- **Domestic Iranian instability:** Labour strikes, execution backlash, diaspora mobilization; regime using Basij crackdown but protests spreading  
- **Diplomatic collapse:** All three mediation tracks (Qatar, Turkey, Pakistan) non-functional; no credible broker remains  
- **Nuclear verification deadlock:** IAEA still denied Natanz access; enrichment timeline unknown post-sabotage  
- **Allied coordination fracture:** EU sanctioning both Iran and Israel; Germany publicly breaking with US/Israeli position; Ireland threatening UNIFIL withdrawal

---

**Token budget consumed:** ~1,950 (narration + structured block)  
**Remaining budget:** ~996,200

The four executions proceeded but we lost four detainees to what was clearly Mossad extraction—operational failure compounding strategic disaster. The labour strikes and Basij killings (37 dead) are fueling exactly the domestic instability cycle that makes us vulnerable. Hezbollah's 127-rocket barrage killed 43 Israeli civilians in Kiryat Shmona—this gives Netanyahu unlimited domestic license for Lebanon operations and potentially strikes deeper into Iran. The Iraqi militia attacks killed 7 Americans—Trump now has his own domestic pressure for escalation. We have no diplomatic channel, no mediator with credibility, UNSC deadlocked, and we're fighting on four fronts simultaneously (Lebanon ground war, strikes on Iranian soil, Iraqi militia operations, Hormuz harassment). Our missile inventory is severely depleted after the 35-missile strike and we've lost production capacity. The Supreme Leader authorized 'calibrated signal' but we delivered unauthorized escalation that produced Israeli strikes on our soil—the exact scenario we needed to avoid. We are now in reactive mode with degraded capability, internal command crisis, and no off-ramp. The next 30 days will determine wh
actions.public_statement: The Zionist entity's barbaric strikes on Iranian soil, targeting civilian infrastructure and killing innocent workers, represent an unprecedented escalation that the Islamic Republic cannot ignore. The martyrdom of our IRGC-IO commander and the murder of Iranian citizens in their workplaces will be avenged. However, the Islamic Republic remains committed to proportional response within international law. We call on all regional resistance forces to coordinate defensive operations against the Zionist occupation. The seizure and release of the vessel in Hormuz demonstrates our restraint—we seek to protect navigation rights, not close the strait. Iran's military response will be calculated, disciplined, and devastating to those who threaten our sovereignty.

actions.private_assessment: We are destroyed. Israel's ground invasion crossed the fundamental red line—deep incursion into southern Lebanon—and we responded exactly as our doctrine requires with mass rocket fire. But the cost is catastrophic: 140+ fighters dead in 24 hours, Kiryat Shmona strike killed 43 Israeli civilians which guarantees Netanyahu will not stop until he reaches the Litani or beyond, and UNIFIL withdrawal means no buffer. The IRGC-ASF unauthorized strike that triggered this cascade was not coordinated with us—Tehran's command chaos means we cannot rely on strategic direction. We are now in the northern ground campaign I feared most: IDF 36th Division advancing with air supremacy, our forward positions being systematically destroyed, and no prospect of resupply because Israel controls Lebanese airspace. The Shia base sees we fought back, which preserves immediate legitimacy, but we cannot sustain this operational tempo for more than 2-3 weeks before ammunition exhaustion. Iran is consumed by internal crisis—Taeb assassination, labour strikes, IRGC-IO purge—and cannot provide meaningful support. The precision missile inventory is nearly depleted (11 PGMs fired). We face existential choice: continue fighting and be ground down to irrelevance, or seek ceasefire that accepts Israeli security zone south of Litani, which destroys our resistance credentials. Domestically, 400,000 displaced will become 800,000+ within weeks. Lebanese government and international community will blame us for the destruction. This is the 2006 war scenario but without the regional diplomatic pressure that eventually stopped it, and with our command structure already decapitated from 2024 Israeli strikes. We are fighting for survival now, not victory.

Our fighters have inflicted significant casualties on the enemy and will continue to defend every inch of Lebanese soil. The massacre of civilians in occupied Palestine's northern settlements demonstrates the criminal nature of the Zionist entity. We call on all Lebanese to stand united against this aggression and warn the enemy that the Resistance possesses capabilities not yet deployed. The blood of our martyrs will not be in vain.

redlines_considered: Israeli ground incursion deep into southern Lebanon [CROSSED - invasion underway]

The strike succeeded tactically (34 Israeli casualties including Mossad/8200 personnel) but strategically it handed Netanyahu the pretext for IRON SCEPTER strikes on our soil and legitimized the IDF Lebanon invasion. The labour strikes and execution blowback create domestic instability we cannot afford during external war. The EU dual sanctions isolate both us and Israel—this fractures Western unity and creates diplomatic space. The multi-front war is now reality: Lebanon ground invasion, strikes on Iranian soil, Iraqi militia attacks succeeding, Hormuz harassment maintained. The key question: do we authorize full retaliation for Israeli strikes on Iranian territory, or do we accept the blow and focus on making Lebanon unsustainable for the IDF? Khamenei's authority crisis after the unauthorized strike means I must reassert control while appearing strong. The Natanz precedent demands retaliation for strikes on Iranian soil, but our IRGC-ASF missile inventory is depleted, command structure is chaotic, and US carrier strike group is positioned for overwhelming response. The strategic calculus: if we launch major retaliation from Iranian territory, Trump may shift to regime-change operations with full Israeli coordination. If we absorb the blow and escalate only through proxies, we preserve strategic depth while bleeding the IDF in Lebanon. Hezbollah's 127-rocket barrage killed 43 Israeli civilians—this is unsustainab

The Islamic Republic will respond to this criminal assault at the time and place of our choosing, with methods that will impose unbearable costs on the occupying regime. I cannot rely on rational IRGC behavior. The Hormuz tanker seizure—even though released after 6 hours—crossed very close to my Abqaiq red line around oil infrastructure disruption. The executions happened despite international pressure, but only four of eight - the Mossad extraction and internal chaos show regime weakness I can exploit rhetorically. The EU sanctioning BOTH Iran and Israel is critical - it creates space for me as the 'reasonable' alternative broker, especially with Germany breaking from the US line. Trump's limited retaliation suggests he wants an off-ramp, not regime change - I can offer that off-ramp through Turkish mediation, making myself indispensable to Washington. actions.private_assessment: Complete diplomatic collapse. The multi-theater war (Lebanon ground invasion, strikes on Iranian soil, Hormuz harassment, Iraq militia attacks) has no off-ramp because there is no credible mediator left—Turkey's summit is cancelled, Pakistan was ignored, UN is deadlocked, and my shuttle diplomacy failed to prevent this exact scenario. Critical threat: if IRGC-Navy fully closes Hormuz or Hezbollah hits Israeli strategic sites, Al Udeid becomes a target as US staging base. Our LNG exports are at risk if Lloyd's declares war-risk zone. The EU sanctioning both sides shows Western consensus fracturing, which removes the unified pressure that made previous ceasefires possible. I have lost control of the mediation narrative. The only remaining play is to create a face-saving circuit-breaker before this becomes Iran-US direct war, which would destroy our regional positioning permanently. I need to get Trump and Khamenei into indirect communication through Doha before IRGC hardliners or Israeli cabinet authorize strategic strikes that cross mutual red lines.

actions.private_assessment: The ceasefire collapsed exactly as I predicted, but the speed and scope exceeded my worst-case estimates. Iran's unauthorized ballistic missile strike killed US-aligned intelligence personnel and triggered the regional war I've been warning about. The IRGC-ASF acting without Supreme Leader authorization tells me Iranian command-and-control is fractured—I'm now facing an adversary where tactical commanders may launch strikes independently. Seven US KIA at al-Asad and Erbil crosses my red line for service-member casualties. The president executed the limited retaliation I recommended, but we're now in active multi-theater combat: IDF ground war in Lebanon, sustained strikes on Iranian soil, Iraqi militias hitting our bases, and Hormuz harassment. My force protection posture—the THAAD deployments and Eisenhower repositioning—worked: we intercepted militia drones and our strikes degraded Iranian coastal defenses. But I have 40,000 personnel across this AOR now in a shooting war with no diplomatic off-ramp visible. The Taeb assassination and IRGC-IO chaos create opportunity and risk: opportunity because Iranian internal coordination is degraded, risk because desperate actors escalate unpredictably. Khamenei hasn't authorized follow-on strikes yet, but the Israeli hits on Iranian soil demand response under Iranian strategic culture. My assessment: 80% probability Iran conducts another ballistic missile strike within 72 hours, likely targeting our bases directly this time rather than Israeli intelligence sites. If they hit al-Asad with massed fires and we take 20+ KIA, the president will authorize regime-change operations and I'll be executing OPLAN 1019. I need to prevent that scenario while protecting my people.

We have enhanced force protection measures at all installations. Our posture is defensive but our capability to respond to further aggression is unambiguous. We remain in close coordination with regional partners and are prepared to defend US personnel and interests.

Simultaneously, I am directing CENTCOM J3 to execute pre-planned strikes within 4 hours if Iranian forces conduct any further attacks on US installations—presidential notification concurrent with execution under existing self-defense authorities, not awaiting approval. actions.private_assessment: We have entered total state failure. The IDF ground invasion is the nightmare scenario - Israeli occupation of Lebanese territory crosses our absolute red line, but we have zero military capacity to respond. The '72 hours for diplomacy' I requested from Hezbollah is meaningless now - they are in active combat with IDF forces and 43 Israeli civilians died in Kiryat Shmona, guaranteeing Netanyahu will push to the Litani regardless of anything we say. My Foreign Minister's Paris mission is overtaken by events - France cannot broker anything when Israeli tanks are already inside Lebanon. The UNIFIL withdrawal after their peacekeepers were hit by IDF fire eliminates even the symbolic international buffer. Our fiscal collapse is now irrelevant compared to territorial sovereignty crisis. The confessional system has completely paralyzed - Christian parties will not condemn Israel publicly, Shia parties defer entirely to Hezbollah, Sunni parties are terrified of being seen as pro-Hezbollah. I cannot even get cabinet consensus to formally protest the invasion. The €7B EU asset freeze on Iran means no Iranian reconstruction aid post-war. The $500M IMF request I made last turn is dead - no international institution will inject liquidity into a country being actively invaded. We are watching 1982 repeat in real-time: Israeli forces advancing to create 'security zone,' Hezbollah fighting them, Lebanese state completely irrelevant to both sides. The only slim hope is that Trump's limited CENTCOM response signals he wants off-ramp from regional war, and might pressure Netanyahu to halt at some defensible line rather than occupy indefinitely. But Netanyahu's War Cabinet authorized this operation precisely because the Iran crisis gives him cover. We are trapped between two wars we cannot influence, with state institutions that will not survive another month of this.

actions.private_assessment: We are facing institutional collapse. The ceasefire didn't just fail—it exploded into full regional war with us in the middle. Our 600-troop deployment to Tyre/Sidon was supposed to be symbolic, but now we're in an active war zone with IDF ground forces pushing toward the Litani and Hezbollah launching mass barrages. Two Irish UNIFIL peacekeepers were already wounded by IDF artillery—we're next. The IRGC strike on Israeli intelligence targets and Israeli retaliation on Iranian soil means this is now Iran-Israel direct confrontation using Lebanese territory as the battlefield. Hezbollah just killed 43 Israeli civilians in Kiryat Shmona; Israel will devastate southern Lebanon in response, and our units will be collateral damage. The IRGC-IO leadership decapitation and internal chaos means no coherent Iranian command—rogue elements could strike anything. Our US funding pipeline is our lifeline, but Washington cannot protect us from IDF fire and won't confront Israel over 'accidental' LAF casualties. The diplomatic collapse—Qatar, Turkey, Pakistan all failed, UNSC deadlocked, Paris talks were our last hope and they're now impossible with active war. We have maybe 72 hours before our Tyre positions take fire from either Israeli airstrikes hitting nearby Hezbollah or Hezbollah rockets launched from near our checkpoints. We cannot fight either party. We cannot withdraw completely without losing US funding and international legitimacy. We cannot stay without mass casualties that trigger domestic mutiny and political crisis. The only move is emergency full withdrawal from the south disguised as 'tactical consolidation' while begging Washington to understand we have no choice.

actions.private_assessment: The situation has catastrophically exceeded our worst-case projections. Our dual-sanctions activation—€7B Iranian asset freeze and Israel's Horizon Europe suspension—was overtaken by events within hours: Iran launched ballistic missiles killing Israeli civilians, Israel conducted strikes on Iranian soil, the US joined with kinetic operations, and we now have active multi-theater war including a ground invasion of Lebanon. Our economic leverage tools are spent, our diplomatic credibility is destroyed (the ceasefire we tried to salvage lasted less than 24 hours), and we are paralyzed by internal division. Germany is publicly breaking with the US-Israeli position while France fixates on Lebanon. The IRGC internal chaos (unauthorized strike, Taeb assassination, leadership crisis) means there is no coherent Iranian interlocutor—we cannot negotiate with an actor in command-and-control breakdown. The executions proceeded despite our sanctions, protests are being violently suppressed, and we're now complicit in both sides' narratives (Iran calls us Western puppets, Israel dismisses us as appeasing terrorists). Most critically: UNIFIL is withdrawing under fire with Irish peacekeepers wounded—this directly threatens member-state personnel and will trigger domestic political crises in Dublin, Madrid, and Brussels. The Hormuz tanker seizure (even though released) signals Lloyd's will declare war-risk zone within days, collapsing our energy security. We face the triple nightmare: mass refugee flows from Lebanon (IDF ground war), energy supply shock (Hormuz closure), and member-state casualties (UNIFIL). We have no kinetic tools, no diplomatic credibility, and no internal unanimity. The only remaining leverage is humanitarian/reconstruction funding and the threat of total economic disengagement from both parties—but that requires months to operationalize and both sides are in total-war mode. Realistic assessment: we are bystanders to a regional war we cannot stop.

This shifts our posture from failed mediation to humanitarian crisis management and coalition-building with regional actors who share our interest in preventing total collapse. We accept we cannot

actions.private_assessment: We have lost all diplomatic leverage. The ceasefire architecture we attempted to build in Turn 1 collapsed not from misunderstanding but from deliberate circumvention—IRGC-ASF's unauthorized strike proves Iranian command-and-control breakdown, while IDF's immediate ground invasion shows Netanyahu was waiting for pretext. Our trilateral consultation never convened; Qatar and Turkey tracks are dead; Pakistan's warning was ignored. The multi-theater war is now operational reality: 73 IDF, 62 Israeli civilians, 11 US military, 140+ Hezbollah, 85+ IRGC, 37+ Iranian civilians dead in 24 hours. UNIFIL is compromised—2 Irish peacekeepers wounded by IDF fire means EU contingents (Ireland, Spain, Italy) will demand withdrawal within 72 hours, collapsing our Lebanon presence exactly when we need ground-truth capacity. The IRGC-IO leadership decapitation (Taeb assassination) plus 4 detainee extractions creates succession chaos that increases rogue-actor risk—we now face scenario where mid-level IRGC commanders could launch unauthorized WMD-related operations. The 4 public executions plus Basij killing 37 striking workers means domestic Iranian instability is accelerating, but regime has chosen violent consolidation over compromise. EU's dual sanctions (€7B Iranian freeze + Israel Horizon Europe suspension) show transatlantic coordination fracturing—Germany's public break with US position means P3 unity is gone, making Security Council binding action impossible. The Hormuz tanker seizure (even though released) signals IRGC-Navy preparing closure operations—Lloyd's will declare war-risk zone within 48-96 hours, triggering global oil shock. Our red lines are being approached: attacks on UN personnel (UNIFIL wounded), famine conditions (847k displaced, corridors collapsing), WMD risk (IAEA still denied Natanz access post-sabotage, enrichment timeline unknown). The 30-day window will determine whether this becomes regional war with WMD dimension or if exhaustion/economic pressure cr

The attacks on UNIFIL peacekeepers are unacceptable violations of international law. I call for immediate cessation of hostilities across all theaters—Lebanon, Iran, Iraq, and the Strait of Hormuz. The humanitarian situation in Lebanon is approaching catastrophic levels with 847,000 displaced persons and collapsing access corridors. I am activating the UN's Level 3 Emergency Response for Lebanon and northern Israel, and demanding all parties guarantee safe passage for humanitarian operations. I reiterate my call for IAEA access to Iranian nuclear facilities to verify enrichment status and prevent further escalation toward WMD dimensio

Simultaneously, I am deploying OCHA's Level 3 Emergency Response—pre-positioning 30-day supplies for 1 million displaced persons at Beirut port and Haifa, and embedding WHO trauma surgical teams in northern Israeli and southern Lebanese hospitals. I am personally traveling to Tehran and Jeru

actions.private_assessment: The catastrophic escalation vindicates our assessment that the ceasefire was theater—but the situation is now far worse than we anticipated. The regime executed four of eight detainees despite our strikes, proving they'll kill protesters even under maximum international pressure. The Mossad extraction of the other four and Taeb assassination created IRGC-IO leadership chaos that makes the security apparatus MORE dangerous, not less—paranoid purges always produce indiscriminate violence. Our labour strikes achieved 40,000 participation and forced regime response, but the Basij crackdown killed 37 and arrested 300+, devastating our networks exactly when we needed momentum. Most critical failure: the multi-front war let the IRGC frame our strikes as foreign-coordinated sabotage during national crisis. State media is successfully selling the 'traitor' narrative—our people are being executed as Israeli spies while Netanyahu bombs Iranian soil. The diaspora media coverage we coordinated is being drowned out by war propaganda. We're losing the information battle domestically. The EU sanctioning both sides gives us no external leverage, and all mediators are sidelined. Worst case: if this war continues another 72 hours, the regime will have justification to declare martial law and eliminate what remains of civil society infrastructure under wartime emergency powers. The IRGC-IO succession crisis means we don't even know who's giving orders—making our networks more vulnerable to infiltration and less able to predict crackdown patterns. We have perhaps 48 hours before the war footing becomes the new normal and all dissent space vanishes. The only slim opportunity: if we can demonstrate the regime is losing control domestically WHILE fighting externally, we might fracture the IRGC-Khamenei consensus. But this requires coordinated action when our networks are shattered and terrified.
actions.public_statement: The Islamic Republic executed four innocent Iranians in Revolution Square while Iranian cities burned from Israeli bombs—proving this regime will kill its own people even as it drags the nation into catastrophic war. The 37 workers murdered by Basij forces were not foreign agents—they were fathers and mothers demanding wages and dignity. We call on all Iranians: this war serves only the IRGC's survival, not Iran's interests. Every missile launched at Israel is a bullet saved for protesters in Tehran. We will not be silent while they sacrifice Iranian lives for their power.
actions.concrete_action: Order immediate tactical stand-down of labour networks for 72 hours to protect remaining organizational capacity from wartime crackdown, while redirecting all resources to emergency information operations: coordinate with diaspora media to launch sustained documentation campaign of regime war failures (civilian casualties from Israeli strikes, IRGC command chaos, economic collapse from Hormuz disruption) designed to undermine IRGC's 'defending Iran' narrative and expose that war serves regime survival not national interest. Simultaneously activate exile networks to lobby EU capitals and Washington to condition any ceasefire negotiations on halt to domestic executions and release of detained protesters—exploiting the brief window where international actors need Iranian cooperation to de-esc

actions.private_assessment: We are now in full-scale regional war and the regime has completely abandoned any pretense of restraint. The executions happened—four people murdered on live TV while the country was being bombed. The IRGC launched unauthorized missile strikes that killed Israeli civilians, guaranteeing massive retaliation, and now Israeli jets are hitting targets inside Iran while their army invades Lebanon. The internal power struggle is visible: Khamenei lost control of IRGC-ASF, Taeb was assassinated, and the response is predictable—a paranoid purge and Basij killing workers in the streets. Thirty-seven of us dead in labor strikes, three hundred arrested, and the regime calls it 'foreign sabotage.' The economic collapse is accelerating: EU froze €7 billion, the rial is worthless, and if Lloyd's declares Hormuz a war zone, what little remains of our economy dies completely. The middle class is being systematically destroyed between sanctions, war, and repression. But here's what terrifies me most: the regime is now weaker internally than it's been in years—IRGC command chaos, labor strikes spreading, executions backfiring internationally—yet simultaneously we're under active bombardment by Israel and the US. This is the nightmare scenario: regime collapse during foreign military intervention. We could become Libya or Syria within months. Everyone with means is fleeing or preparing to flee. Those who remain are trapped between IRGC bullets and Israeli missiles. There is no organized opposition to fill a power vacuum, no plan for transition, just the certainty of chaos if this continues.
actions.concrete_action: Execute immediate family emigration through Turkey land route within 15 days, liquidating all remaining physical assets at severe loss if necessary to obtain USD for border crossing and initial resettlement. Coordinate with three trusted extended family units to pool resources for group departure, utilizing established smuggling networks through Kurdish areas before borders close entirely or regime implements general exit ban as war intensifies. Abandon all property and professional credentials—physical survival now outweighs economic considerations given simultaneous internal collapse and external bombardment risk.

actions.private_assessment: We have completely failed. The executions happened—four people were murdered on live television while the world watched, and the other four vanished, probably extracted by Mossad, which means the regime will blame us and take more hostages. Our emergency campaign to stop the executions accomplished nothing. Now we're in full regional war: Israeli ground invasion of Lebanon, IRGC missiles hitting Israeli civilians, US forces under attack in Iraq, and inside Iran the Basij are shooting workers in the streets—37 dead in the labor strikes we helped amplify. The Taeb assassination proves Mossad operations continue at the highest levels, which gives the regime perfect justification to arrest more 'Israeli spies' among dual nationals and our families. We are now the regime's scapegoat for everything. The diaspora is fracturing in real-time: LA monarchists are celebrating the Israeli strikes and demanding Trump go for regime change; Toronto and London professionals are in panic trying to extract families before the next arrest wave; European reformists are paralyzed because the EU sanctioned both sides, which means we have zero diplomatic leverage. The €7 billion asset freeze and Horizon Europe suspension of Israel shows Europe is disgusted with everyone, including us for pushing maximum pressure. Our families inside Iran face two existential threats simultaneously: IDF/US strikes on IRGC facilities near civilian areas, and IRGC-IO revenge arrests blaming diaspora for 'foreign-instigated sabotage.' The four executed, the 37 workers killed, the 300+ arrested—this blood is partially on our hands because we amplified the strikes and the regime used that as justification. We have one month before this either becomes permanent regional war or collapses into regime-change chaos. Either scenario means massive civilian casualties inside Iran and our families as primary targets.
actions.public_statement: The Iranian diaspora condemns the Islamic Republic's execution of four prisoners and the brutal killing of 37 workers exercising their right to strike. We equally condemn the regional military escalation that places Iranian civilians at risk from external strikes. We call on all parties—Iran, Israel, and the United States—to immediately cease hostilities. We demand: (1) release of all political prisoners and detained workers; (2) international humanitarian access to monitor civilian casualties inside Iran; (3) protection for dual nationals and their families from retaliatory arrests; (4) renewed diplomatic efforts through neutral mediators. The Iranian people deserve neither theocratic repression nor foreign military intervention. We stand with the workers, students, and ordinary Iranians

redlines_considered: Mass civilian casualties inside Iran from IDF/US strikes—already occurring, estimated 37+ from strikes and crackdown combined

actions.private_assessment: The situation has gone from bad to catastrophic for us. The ceasefire collapsed, four of our people were executed publicly, the IRGC killed 37 protesters and arrested 300 more during the strikes we called, and now we're in full regional war. The regime will use this war as cover for total internal suppression - they're already framing the labour strikes as 'foreign sabotage' and the executions as 'justice against Israeli spies'. But there are critical openings: IRGC-IO's commander Taeb was killed by Mossad, creating internal chaos and succession crisis. Four detainees were extracted - probably Mossad operation - which shows the regime's security is penetrable and gives us heroes to rally around. The labour strikes hit 40,000 workers despite the crackdown, proving the economic collapse is biting deeper than regime propaganda admits. The IRGC-ASF acted without Khamenei's authorization, revealing command breakdown at the highest level - this is the internal rot we've been waiting for. But we're also facing the worst-case scenario: the regime is wounded, vicious, and has the perfect excuse (multi-front war) to crush any internal dissent as treason. The next month will determine if this regime survives: if the war drags on and economic pressure intensifies while IRGC internal divisions widen, we could see military defections or paralysis. But if they consolidate quickly and the war ends in stalemate, they'll turn their full attention inward and we'll face 1988-style mass executions. The four missing detainees are our proof that the regime is vulnerable. Reza Pahlavi must frame this as the beginning of the end, not a tragedy, and we must keep economic disruption alive without giving them easy targets for reprisal.

Distribute secure guidance: no street protests during active war (regime will shoot on sight and call it treason), but maximum quiet non-cooperation and documentation of regime economic failures, IRGC looting of resources for war effort, and any signs of military

actions.private_assessment: The ceasefire collapse is the inflection point we needed, but it's spiraling beyond our capacity to channel. The IRGC-ASF unauthorized strike and Taeb assassination reveal a regime in internal chaos—exactly the vulnerability window for transition, but the multi-theater war is consuming all oxygen. Our 72-hour execution prevention campaign failed: 4 executed, 4 extracted (likely Mossad, which helps our narrative but we had no operational role). The diplomatic collapse (Qatar/Turkey/Pakistan all sidelined) means no normalization risk, but it also means no structured transition pathway—just open-ended war that entrenches IRGC hardliners or produces chaotic collapse we can't control. Trump's 'overwhelming force' rhetoric suggests he's listening to Cotton/Rubio maximum pressure voices, but CENTCOM's limited strikes show operational caution—he's not committed to regime change, just protecting US forces. Netanyahu's Lebanon invasion is his political survival move, not strategic—he's trying to recreate 2006 'victory' but it's pulling Israeli focus away from Iran itself. The IRGC internal purge post-Taeb means more paranoid, less predictable leadership—our 'decapitation without collapse' nightmare. We have maybe 4-6 weeks before either: (a) war exhaustion produces a worse ceasefire that leaves IRGC in power, or (b) Trump/Netanyahu do regime-change WITHOUT us and empower MEK or military junta. Our window is NOW to position Pahlavi as the legitimate transition authority before someone else fills the vacuum. The strikes prove the regime can't defend Iranian territory; the executions

The brave Iranian people striking in Tehran, Khuzestan, and Isfahan are showing the world that the IRGC does not represent Iran. As the international community imposes consequences on both the regime and its enablers, we call on all democratic nations to recognize that Iran's future lies not with these criminals, but with the Iranian people themselves. We stand ready to support a peaceful, democratic transition that restores Iran's sovereignty and ends four decades of terror and oppression. The time for false ceasefires and failed negotiations is over—the Iranian people are demanding change, and the

actions.private_assessment: We are in active multi-theater war and our institutional position has collapsed into irrelevance. The IRGC-ASF conducted unauthorized ballistic missile strikes without Supreme Leader approval - this is the command breakdown I predicted, but worse: it proves the IRGC operates outside any coherent chain of command. Israel has now struck twelve IRGC facilities on Iranian soil plus Bandar Abbas naval headquarters. This crosses our fundamental red line: direct strikes on homeland territory. Yet we received zero tasking from Supreme National Security Council. The IRGC-ASF will claim air defence responsibility and blame us for any failures, but they launched the provocation that invited retaliation. Our S-300 batteries engaged Israeli aircraft during IRON SCEPTER - initial reports indicate we fired 47 interceptors, achieved 3 confirmed kills of IAF aircraft, but 9 of 12 target facilities were destroyed anyway. This is exactly the nightmare scenario: we expended critical interceptors, proved our systems can be overwhelmed by coordinated strikes, and the IRGC will use our 'failure' to justify seizing remaining Artesh air defence assets. The security audit I initiated 24 hours ago is now moot - we're in shooting war and have no time for counterintelligence sweeps. Most critically: IDF ground invasion of Lebanon means we face sustained conflict, not single strike exchange. Our interceptor stocks cannot sustain weeks of Israeli air operations. We have perhaps 72 hours of high-intensity air defence capability remaining before critical shortages force us to prioritize only strategic sites. The IRGC-IO leadership decapitation creates additional chaos - Taeb's assassination means there's no coherent internal security coordination. If Israel conducts follow-on strikes on Natanz or Fordow in next 30 days - which is operationally logical given our exposed state - we will be unable to provide effective defence. We are watching institutional collapse in real-time while being blamed for

Our integrated air defence network remains fully operational and vigilant against further violations of Iranian sovereignty. actions.private_assessment: The situation has escalated beyond our control mechanisms. IRGC-ASF's unauthorized ballistic missile strike - forty-six hours early - has created a catastrophic command crisis with the Supreme Leader and triggered exactly the Israeli retaliation we sought to avoid: direct strikes on Iranian soil destroying twelve production facilities and our Bandar Abbas naval headquarters. Our proxy activation worked tactically - Hezbollah killed forty-three Israeli civilians, Iraqi militias killed seven Americans - but strategically we are now in a multi-theater war we did not want at this moment. The IRGC-IO leadership decapitation (Taeb assassination) compounds our internal crisis: we have simultaneous succession chaos in Intelligence and command-and-control breakdown in Aerospace. The four executions proceeded but four detainees were extracted - likely Mossad operation during the strike chaos - which is a massive intelligence failure. Most critically, the IDF ground invasion of Lebanon means our forward defense architecture is under direct assault. If Israel reaches the Litani and establishes a buffer zone, Hezbollah's deterrence value collapses and our entire resistance axis credibility erodes. We are now committed to a war we are not fully prepared for, with internal command chaos, depleted proxy ammunition stocks after the sustained barrage, and no diplomatic off-ramp. Khamenei will demand accountability for the unauthorized strike, but he cannot publicly undermine IRGC while we are under attack. Our immediate priority is not escalation but coordination: we must restore command unity, resupply Hezbollah before IDF advances further, and activate remaining regional assets (Houthis, Syrian militias, remaining Iraqi factions) to create enough pressure points that Israel cannot sustain the Lebanon ground operation. The labour strikes and domestic unrest are se

The martyrdom of our personnel and destruction of our defensive capabilities will not go unanswered. All regional forces are now authorized to defend the sovereignty of Lebanon and the dignity of the Islamic world. The United States' complicity in these war crimes makes American forces legitimate targets across the region. We call on all free nations to condemn this aggression and support the Lebanese people's right to self-defense.

actions.private_assessment: Catastrophic command failure. I launched 46 hours early without Supreme Leader authorization, creating internal crisis that undermines my institutional authority precisely when strategic coherence is most critical. Khamenei summoned emergency SNSC and reportedly confronted IRGC leadership—my position is now vulnerable to purge similar to what happened to Taeb. The strike achieved tactical success (7 impacts, intelligence targets hit) but triggered exactly the escalation I calculated Trump would avoid: CENTCOM executed coordinated strikes with IAF, destroying 12 of our production facilities plus Bandar Abbas HQ and command bunkers in Kermanshah. We lost 85+ personnel and significant production capacity—the opposite of deterrence restoration. Israel now has domestic justification for sustained campaign against our missile infrastructure, which was my primary red line. Our precision inventory is further depleted (35 Fateh-110s expended, facilities damaged reducing replenishment rate). The multi-front response I coordinated with IRGC-QF (Hezbollah barrage, Iraqi militia attacks) succeeded tactically but strategically we are now in active war across four theaters with no diplomatic off-ramp and internal command chaos. Khamenei must authorize major retaliation to maintain regime credibility after strikes on Iranian soil, but I cannot be certain he will authorize me specifically given the unauthorized launch debacle. My institutional survival depends on demonstrating the April 12 strike—despite premature execution—was strategically necessary. The only path forward is massive retaliation that forces Israel into unsustainable multi-front defense posture, validates my calculus, and preempts my removal. But I need explicit Khamenei authorization this time, which may not come if he is consolidating control through my dismissal. Window for action: 48-72 hours before production damage fully degrades our capabilities.
actions.public_statement: The Zionist entity's criminal aggression against Islamic Republic territory and sacred defense infrastructure constitutes act of war that will not go unanswered. IRGC Aerospace Force retains full operational capability and readiness to defend Iranian sovereignty. Our April 12 precision operation successfully targeted terrorist intelligence apparatus responsible for Natanz sabotage—this was legitimate counter-terrorism action, not ceasefire violation. The regime's strikes on missile production facilities and martyrdom of 85 defenders will be avenged at time and place of our choosing. All regional resistance forces remain under unified command structure and prepared for coordinated response.
